title: `--platform` argument in Dockerfile is silently ignored
body:
### Issue Description

When building an image from a Dockerfile that contains the `--platform` argument, the argument is ignored completely and no error is displayed. The resulting image is simply constructed as if no argument was given (i.e. the default arch is used).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a Dockerfile thats just `FROM --platform=linux/arm64 ubuntu`
2. Run `podman build . --arch amd64` once to make sure the "default arch" is `amd64` and not `arm64`
3. Run `podman build .`


### Describe the results you received

The second image is still `amd64`


### Describe the results you expected

Either:
- The second image should be `arm64`
- A warning is displayed stating that the `--platform` argument is not supported

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

See https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#from for the --platform argument

comments.body: You are using an ancient podman version, if you use a newer one it should work
